methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Steady Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Tommi Nyman, Hannes Haapalahti, and Petteri Tenhunen, currently valued at $110M post-money. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public (LNZA) and 8 private firms at Series B and adjacent stages, including Commonwealth Fusion Systems, Pano AI, Sage Geosystems, Reverion, and CarbonCapture Inc.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, anchoring to the $110M last post-money valuation and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, implies a valuation of $220M. Peer-median analysis across the 8 same-sector private comparables yields a median valuation of $305M, with a range spanning $120M to $5.0B. The wide peer range reflects heterogeneity in technology maturity and funding momentum within frontier energy, particularly the outlier position of Commonwealth Fusion Systems at $5.0B.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $187M (low) to $351M (high), with a median of $262M. This implies meaningful upside from Steady Energy's current $110M valuation, though materialization depends critically on execution risk and market conditions. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of recent funding velocity in climate tech, differentiation relative to capital-intensive competitors, and path to defensible unit economics in hardware-centric segments.
